name: Kui Xing
primary_sources: Records of Daily[-gained] Knowledge by Gu Yan-Wu
Grand Historian and Astronomer Sima Qian

visual_attributes.morphology: Bent and hunchbacked figure resembling a calligraphy character
distinctive_features: Right foot standing on a giant turtle or fish
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Asymmetrical posture with one foot on a turtle or fish and the other on an inkpot

domain_specific_visual.AO_REFERENCE: Standing on a giant turtle or fish

domain_specific_functional.EXAMINATION_SUCCESS: Venerated by students for success in Imperial Civil Service exams
domain_specific_functional.YIN_YANG_SPLIT: Associated with splitting Yin and Yang

relationships.description: Assists Wenchang Wang, the deification of Ursa Major

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Unknown, but veneration noted in late Ming Dynasty
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of success in examinations and human fortune
primary_sources: Records of Daily[-gained] Knowledge by Gu Yan-Wu

contrastive_constraints: NOT unrelated to examinations or academic success
